title: AI Chief of Staff Skill-Orchestrated Agent Architecture
summary: Completed the shipped `AI Chief of Staff` root slice: one builder-facing assistant template, one internal skill contract and registry, follow-through/travel/briefing skill adapters, trigger-aware routing for scheduled/message/PubSub wakeups, and runtime cleanup of transient trigger context after each cycle.
next_action: Implement the explicit follow-on spec for shared source-bundle acquisition and assistant-level attention arbitration.

verification.notes: The implementation slice matched the re-scoped canonical spec and the repo passed `mix precommit` with `987 tests, 0 failures` on 2026-04-02. Shared acquisition and assistant-level arbitration were intentionally split into a dedicated follow-on spec instead of being left as vague unfinished checklist items.

resume_context.last_completed_step: Closed the v1 orchestration-root slice and split the remaining acquisition/arbitration work into an explicit follow-on spec.
last_reviewed_sections: 11. Implementation Checklist For The Shipped Slice
resume_context.notes: The user-facing decision for this slice is clear: one primary Chief of Staff assistant, Slack remains bundled into `followthrough`, and deeper acquisition/arbitration work now lives in its own ready spec.

history.note: Initial draft created for a skill-oriented `AI Chief of Staff` architecture that consolidates travel, follow-through, and briefing under one user-facing assistant.

Implementation began for the new `ai_chief_of_staff` behavior, the Chief of Staff skill registry/contract, adapter-based orchestration of the current travel and follow-through modules, and builder support for launching the unified assistant.

Completed the first architecture milestone: the `ai_chief_of_staff` behavior, Chief of Staff skill registry/contract, skill adapters for follow-through/travel/briefing, builder integration, and passing `mix precommit` verification.

Added trigger-aware skill routing for scheduled/message/PubSub cycles and cleared transient runtime trigger context after each behavior cycle so reactive work does not leak into later scheduled wakeups.

Re-scoped the canonical spec to the actually shipped orchestration-root slice, resolved the previously open design questions explicitly, and moved shared acquisition plus assistant-level arbitration into a dedicated follow-on spec.
Closed the v1 `AI Chief of Staff` orchestration-root implementation after final review against the updated canonical spec and a green `mix precommit` gate.
